context_note: Ivory Coast vs Ecuador - the MOST competitive match we've priced yet by draw probability: IVC 29.0% / Draw 33.1% / ECU 37.9% (vig-adj). RULE8 (from JPN-NED postmortem) applied prospectively for the first time, especially to Q1.

player_markets_unresolved: Player 0.5+ SOT (144809807) - Sangare contract ID not resolved in time
Player to score or assist (144809828) - Sarmiento contract ID not resolved in time

ecuador_n4.note: 1 of 5 lastFiveGames events (758717, 2025-11-19) returned no boxscore in any league tried - excluded

q1_ecuador_more_fouls_than_ivory_coast.method: Skellam on historical foul rates: ECU avg=15.0 (n=4), IVC avg=12.0 (n=5), mean_diff=3.0, var=27, z=0.577, raw P=0.718. This is structurally the SAME setup as JPN-NED Q1 (raw 0.764 -> submitted 0.72 -> actual exact tie 7-7, -25.37, our worst result). Draw price here (33.06%) is even HIGHER than JPN-NED (27.9%). Per refined RULE8 (game_dynamics_analysis.md): lean to 0.55-0.60 rather than the raw/sample-shrunk number when draw price is elevated.

q2_ecuador_more_corners_than_ivory_coast.method: ESPN full-match corner avgs nearly identical (IVC 4.8, ECU 4.75) -> 1H-scaled lambdas IVC=2.4, ECU=2.375 -> Skellam with continuity correction for strict inequality ~0.40 (high tie-probability at low HT counts). Nudged to 0.42: full-match corner-handicap market favors Ecuador's side (ECU>=IVC 63.3% vs IVC>ECU 36.7%), and Ecuador is the modest FTR favorite (37.9% vs 29.0%).

q3_ivory_coast_2plus_offsides.method: First-pass Poisson, Ivory Coast offsides n=5 (0,1,2,1,3), lambda=1.4, P(X>=2)=1-e^-1.4*(1+1.4)=0.408. Submitted unshaded per RULE7 (this category's best-performing pattern across all matches).

q4_ecuador_more_2h_sot_than_ivory_coast.method: ESPN full-match SOT: ECU avg=4.25 (n=4), IVC avg=4.4 (n=5) - nearly identical, ECU marginally lower. 2H-scaled Skellam (lambda_ECU=2.125, lambda_IVC=2.2): mean_diff=-0.075, var=4.325, z=-0.036, P(ECU>IVC)=0.486. Near coin-flip, consistent with the high draw probability.

q5_second_half_more_goals_than_first_hal.method: Direct from Smarkets 'Highest scoring half' market, SECOND_HALF contract, vig-adjusted (FIRST_HALF 28.05% / EQUAL 31.82% / SECOND_HALF 40.18%). Undeviated.

q6_ivory_coast_win.method: Direct from Smarkets full-time-result market, vig-adjusted (Ivory Coast 29.01%). RULE1 (avg(crowd,market)) not computable pre-match - treated as tier1_market undeviated per established convention.

q7_ivory_coast_scores_in_2h.method: Direct from Smarkets 'Second half Ivory Coast over/under 0.5 goals' market, OVER contract = scores 1+, vig-adjusted 40.86%. Undeviated.

q8_4plus_total_cards.method: Direct from Smarkets 'Over/under 3.5 cards' market, OVER contract = 4+ cards, vig-adjusted 53.6%. Undeviated.

q9_sangare_1plus_sot.method: TIME-CONSTRAINED ESTIMATE. Smarkets 'Player to have over 0.5 shots on target' market exists (144809807, ~90 contracts) but Sangare's specific contract ID (Smarkets player IDs are opaque numeric params with no name field returned by the contracts endpoint) was not resolved within the submission window. Ibrahim Sangare is a defensive midfielder (low shot-volume role) - estimate based on position/role base rate, lower confidence than usual.

q10_sarmiento_score_or_assist.method: TIME-CONSTRAINED ESTIMATE. Smarkets 'Player to score or assist' market exists (144809828) but Sarmiento's specific contract ID not resolved within the submission window (same issue as Q9). Jeremy Sarmiento is an Ecuador wide attacker but not the clear focal attacking outlet - estimate based on role base rate, lower confidence than usual.

questions.leaderboard_question: Will Ecuador commit more fouls than Ivory Coast?
questions.note: RULE8 n=2: shrunk from raw 0.718 to 0.57. Outcome=YES (ECU 13 fouls vs IVC 10). Beat crowd, but a higher (unshunk) estimate would have scored even better here - shrinkage cost some upside on this occasion (vs JPN-NED where the analogous unshrunk estimate caused -25.37). Net: shrinkage reduces variance without costing the win when right.

questions.leaderboard_question: At halftime, will Ecuador have more corner kicks than Ivory Coast?
Will Ivory Coast be caught offside 2 or more times?
questions.note: Unshaded first-pass Poisson (RULE7) wins again. IVC offsides=0.

questions.leaderboard_question: Will Ecuador have more shots on target than Ivory Coast in the second half?
questions.note: Resolved via commentary: IVC 2H SOT=3 (60',90',90+4'), ECU 2H SOT=1 (68'). RBP approximate (rounded from live 'IF NO' display, exact pending leaderboard refresh).

questions.leaderboard_question: Will the second half have more total goals than the first half?
questions.note: Only goal (Diallo, 90') was in 2H. tier1_market undeviated lost to crowd's near-50% here - low-scoring match with the goal landing in 2H.

questions.leaderboard_question: Will Ivory Coast win the match?
questions.note: IVC won 1-0. Crowd's 34% beat our market-derived 29%. RBP approximate (rounded from live 'IF YES' display, exact pending).

questions.leaderboard_question: Will Ivory Coast score in the second half?
Will there be 4 or more total cards shown?
Will Ibrahim Sangare have at least 1 shot on target?
questions.note: HEADLINE RESULT: Sangare had 0 shots (totalShots=0, shotsOnTarget=0) - confirmed via ESPN athlete boxscore. Our 'time-constrained, low-confidence' 18% was directionally correct and beat crowd's 33% by a wide margin (sq err 0.0324 vs 0.1089) - the highest-stakes question (AT_STAKE=31, asymmetric -23/+8) resolved on the +8 side. RBP approximate (rounded from live 'IF NO' display, exact pending).

post_match_results.q10_status: Not present in the settled leaderboard paste provided by the user (only Q1-Q9 shown) - status of 'Will Jeremy Sarmiento score or assist' unknown. Sum of Q1-Q9 (using exact values for Q1,2,3,5,7,8 and approximate for Q4,6,9) = 21.20 vs authoritative match total 19.86 (diff -1.34, likely rounding in the live IF-YES/IF-NO display values for Q4/Q6/Q9, and/or a Q10 contribution not captured here). Total_rbp field uses the authoritative +19.86 from the Match Summary header.
post_match_results.summary: Cote d'Ivoire won 1-0 (Amad Diallo, 90'). Beat crowd on 5 of 6 fully-confirmed questions (Q1,Q2,Q3,Q4,Q8) plus very likely Q9 (Sangare 0 SOT, our 18% vs crowd's 33% - our best beat-crowd margin this match by squared-error). Lost on Q5 (2H>1H goals, tier1_market undeviated, crowd's near-50% won) and Q6 (IVC win, crowd's 34% beat our market-derived 29%) and Q7 (small loss). RULE8 (n=2 now): the Q1 shrinkage (0.72 raw -> 0.57 submitted) did not cost the win when the outcome went our way (YES) - it only reduces the magnitude of the win slightly, while in JPN-NED's analogous unshrunk case the failure to shrink caused a -25.37 loss. This supports RULE8 as a variance-reduction technique with low cost when right, large benefit when wrong.
